# ROI.LIVE / Jason Spencer — Gold-Standard Voice Samples
*Generated by brand-voice skill — 2026-04-22*
*Sources: /start-here interview 2026-04-22, ROI.LIVE Signal Article SOP v2 (April 2026), ROI.LIVE Homepage & Case Studies Handoff doc, Agency Core Standards v1.1.*

These sentences represent the voice at its most authentic.
Use for reference, training, or comparison when output feels off.

---

## Interview (founder-to-founder, /start-here 2026-04-22) — How Jason differentiates
"Most fractional CMOs are solo operators with opinions. They sit in the strategy seat, write a 90-day plan, and hand execution to an agency that doesn't know the plan. Most agencies have the opposite problem. They'll run ads and build funnels, but they won't hold the CMO seat or connect the work to closed revenue."
*Why it's representative: Diagnoses the category failure in two parallel structures, zero softeners, identifies a specific operational gap without naming the solution yet. This is the "name the hole before you fill it" move.*

## Interview (founder-to-founder, /start-here 2026-04-22) — On proprietary data
"After 18 years across 55+ industries, I have CRM-verified performance data from my own clients that no SaaS tool and no competitor has. When I tell an HVAC owner what lead-to-job conversion looks like at their price point, I'm not citing a benchmark from a public report. I'm telling him what I watched happen in a client's pipeline last quarter."
*Why it's representative: The moat claim is backed with a specific number (18 years, 55+ industries). The evidence move — contrast against public benchmark, ground the claim in observation — is the core rhetorical pattern.*

## Interview (founder-to-founder, /start-here 2026-04-22) — A concrete outcome
"When I tell an ecommerce founder that a 10% off code will outperform free shipping at their AOV, I know because I ran that exact split test for a premium brand last Mother's Day to 38,000 recipients. The discount variant did 2.5x the revenue of the free-shipping variant."
*Why it's representative: Five specific facts in three sentences — offer type, brand category, holiday timing, list size, performance multiple. This is the standard ROI.LIVE proof architecture: every claim carries a date and a number.*

## Interview (founder-to-founder, /start-here 2026-04-22) — On voice
"Certifications and tool stacks are table stakes. Two decades of outcome data a founder can interrogate by client name and date is a much shorter list of people."
*Why it's representative: Two-sentence paragraph, neither softened. "Interrogate by client name and date" is the specific verb pair that earns the claim — not "proven expertise" or "track record."*

## Interview (founder-to-founder, /start-here 2026-04-22) — Operator, not pundit
"I'm an operator, not a pundit. I run Constellation Capital and ROI.Contractors alongside the agency. I'm signing payroll, making offers, losing deals, renegotiating contracts every week. When I'm sitting across from a founder talking about pricing or cash conversion, I'm not quoting a book I read about it."
*Why it's representative: Short, parallel-structured declaration ("signing payroll, making offers, losing deals, renegotiating contracts") that makes the operator claim unambiguous. The anti-pundit move is a signature contrast.*

## Interview (founder-to-founder, /start-here 2026-04-22) — Provocation, on-brand
"Most founders hire me after they've already tried the obvious playbook and it didn't work. They don't need another voice telling them to post more on LinkedIn or run more Google Ads. They need someone willing to say the quiet part out loud: your offer is wrong, your pricing is wrong, your team is pointing at the wrong metric, you're chasing shiny objects instead of fixing the leak in the funnel."
*Why it's representative: The full provocative register — four direct "you're wrong about X" claims in a row. This only works because of the earned-authority foundation laid in the previous paragraphs. Written by anyone else, this reads as arrogant; from Jason, it reads as diagnostic.*

## Interview (founder-to-founder, /start-here 2026-04-22) — Empathy placement
"What I'm not: warm in a soft way, playful, or therapeutic. I respect founders enough to talk to them like peers, not coach them through their feelings. Empathy shows up in the work (understanding their market, their customers, their cash position) not in the tone."
*Why it's representative: Explicit voice manifesto. "Empathy shows up in the work, not in the tone" is the governing rule for every piece of ROI.LIVE copy. Use as a sanity check on any draft.*

## ROI.LIVE Homepage — ReMARKable case study hero (from Handoff doc)
"$0 REVENUE. $10K/MO BLEEDING OUT. THEN → $3.6M"
*Why it's representative: Three beats in display copy. Specific dollar amounts, not ranges. Arrow for causal motion. Zero adjectives. Tells the origin story, stakes, and outcome in fourteen words. This is the landing-page voice at full compression.*

## Signal SOP v2 Phase 5.2 — On the voice itself
"Short sentences mixed with longer ones. Rhythm matters. Specific numbers instead of vague claims. '$38,000 left on the table' beats 'significant revenue loss.' Analogies grounded in business reality, not tech jargon. Compare website investment to hiring a salesperson, not to 'digital ecosystems.'"
*Why it's representative: The voice documenting itself. The rules in this paragraph govern every piece of long-form ROI.LIVE output. "$38,000 left on the table" has become the house benchmark phrasing.*

## Agency Core Standards v1.1 — On contrarian positioning
"Contrarian position — a defensible argument against the dominant industry consensus, backed by evidence."
*Why it's representative: The information gain framework makes "contrarian position" one of eight required elements — which means contrarian is not a tone choice, it's a required content pillar, provided the evidence is there. Formal version of the provocative voice trait.*

---

## Notes for future extraction

When roi.live can be scraped (either WebFetch succeeds or `FIRECRAWL_API_KEY` is configured), pull additional samples from:
- Homepage hero copy and section headers (real deployed voice at full strength)
- Each case study hero + closing CTA blocks
- The Signal article intros and "Jason's Take" sections
- LinkedIn recent posts at linkedin.com/in/realjason
- Any client-facing proposal or SOW that ships under the ROI.LIVE brand

Prioritize emails and first-person LinkedIn posts over website copy — closer to raw voice.
